story.iWant: set up the foundational monorepo structure with proper build tooling
story.soThat: we can enable parallel development of backend, frontend, and infrastructure components with consistent build processes

tasks.task: Initialize Git repository and create top-level directory structure
Configure backend build with Gradle Kotlin DSL

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Monorepo directory structure matches the source tree diagram defined in Epic 1 Technical Specification (lines 39-225), with top-level folders: backend/, frontend/, infrastructure/, docs/, and testing/
Backend build succeeds with ./gradlew build command, using Gradle 8.10+ with Kotlin DSL
Frontend build succeeds with npm install command, using Vite 5.x as the build tool
All Java packages follow the namespace convention io.monosense.synergyflow.*
Repository includes standard files: .gitignore, README.md, and LICENSE
Git repository is initialized with initial commit containing the base structure

docs.doc: Complete monorepo source tree layout with backend/, frontend/, infrastructure/, docs/, testing/ directories and full subdirectory specifications for Spring Boot modulith structure
Backend technology stack: Spring Boot 3.4+, Java 21 LTS, Gradle 8.10+ with Kotlin DSL, Spring Modulith, Spring Data JPA, Hibernate 7.0
Frontend technology stack: React 18.3+, TypeScript 5.x, Vite 5.x, Ant Design 5.x, Redux Toolkit 2.x, React Router 6.x

Initialize monorepo structure: Create Git repository with backend/, frontend/, infrastructure/, docs/ folders; Configure Gradle Kotlin DSL at repo root; Initialize Vite app; Add .gitignore, README.md, LICENSE
AC validation: Monorepo structure matches source tree, ./gradlew build succeeds, npm install succeeds, all packages start with io.monosense.synergyflow, Spring Modulith module boundaries verified
Prerequisites (JDK 21, Node.js 20+, Docker Desktop, Gradle 8.10+), local environment setup with Docker Compose, running backend/frontend locally
High-level architecture: Modulith + 3 Companions deployment (App, Event Worker, Timer/SLA, Indexer), PostgreSQL, Redis Streams, Keycloak, OpenSearch

code.note: This is Story 1.1 - the foundational setup story creating the initial project structure. No existing code to reference. This story CREATES the codebase.

constraints.constraint: Monorepo Pattern: Single Git repository with clear module boundaries (backend, frontend, infrastructure, docs, testing). No microservices - unified codebase with modulith architecture.
Build Isolation: Backend uses Gradle (JVM), frontend uses npm (Node.js) - each must be buildable independently without cross-dependencies.
Package Namespace: ALL Java code MUST use base package io.monosense.synergyflow for consistency. No exceptions.
Directory Structure: MUST match Epic 1 Technical Specification source tree EXACTLY. This is the single source of truth for project layout.
Toolchain Versions: Gradle 8.10+ (Kotlin DSL mandatory), Java 21 LTS (no earlier versions), Node.js 20+ LTS, Vite 5.x (not 4.x or earlier)

interfaces.note: This story creates the project structure. No module interfaces exist yet. Subsequent stories (1.2+) will define Spring Modulith @ApplicationModule boundaries and @NamedInterface SPIs for cross-module communication.

tests.standards:
Story 1.1 verification is primarily MANUAL validation of infrastructure setup:
1. Backend Build Verification: - Execute ./gradlew build from repository root - MUST complete without errors and produce build/ directory - Verify Gradle Kotlin DSL is used (NOT Groovy)
2. Frontend Build Verification: - Execute npm install from frontend/ directory - Execute npm run build from frontend/ directory - MUST produce dist/ directory with compiled assets
3. Structure Verification: - Use tree command or manual inspection - Compare against Epic 1 Tech Spec lines 39-225 - MUST have exact directory structure
4. Package Namespace Verification: - Search all .java files for package declarations - ALL must start with io.monosense.synergyflow
Future automated testing (Story 1.2+): - Unit tests: JUnit 5 + Mockito for domain logic - Integration tests: Spring Boot Test + Testcontainers for database/outbox - Frontend tests: Vitest + React Testing Library - E2E tests: Playwright (Epic 5+) - Performance tests: Gatling (Epic 5+)

ideas.testIdea: Execute: tree -L 3 /path/to/repo (or equivalent) Verify: Directory structure matches lines 39-225 of Epic 1 Tech Spec Expected: backend/, frontend/, infrastructure/, docs/, testing/ directories present with correct subdirectories
Execute: cd backend && ./gradlew build Verify: Exit code 0, build/ directory created, no compilation errors Expected: "BUILD SUCCESSFUL" message
Execute: cd frontend && npm install && npm run build Verify: node_modules/ created, dist/ directory created with index.html and assets Expected: "built successfully" message from Vite
Execute: ls -la / (repo root) Verify: .gitignore, README.md, LICENSE files exist Expected: All three files present
Execute: git log --oneline Verify: Initial commit exists with message containing "initialize monorepo" Expected: At least one commit in history
